### Post by matt952 on 2014-06-13
I have the same problem, I installed 3 different distros (14.04, 13.10, 13.04) and the problem still remains. The browser loads only some pages but not many others related to Google. I've also tried to change the DNS but this didn't help at all. That's my output of what steeldriver suggested

```
$ [COLOR=#000000]cat /etc/resolv.conf[/COLOR]
# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
nameserver 127.0.1.1

$ sudo lsof -i :53
COMMAND  PID   USER   FD   TYPE DEVICE SIZE/OFF NODE NAME
dnsmasq 1540 nobody    4u  IPv4    851      0t0  UDP matt95-desktop:domain 
dnsmasq 1540 nobody    5u  IPv4    852      0t0  TCP matt95-desktop:domain (LISTEN)
```

### Post by steeldriver on 2014-06-13
[I]@matt952 I have moved your post to its own thread so it doesn't get lost in the back-and-forth

[/I]Loading **some** pages is usually a bit different from not even being able to resolve hostnames - can you try editing your network connection to use a lower MTU?
